---
title: "Research"
---

## Overview

My research centers on the mechanics, durability, and failure of advanced composite materials and structures under coupled environmental and mechanical loading. I combine experimental characterization, constitutive understanding, and finite element simulation to reveal degradation mechanisms and improve structural design.

## Main Research Themes

### 1. Environmental durability of CFRP composites

I study how seawater, moisture, temperature, and hydrostatic pressure affect the damage evolution and load-bearing capacity of composite laminates. This line of research aims to provide a more realistic understanding of composite service behavior in marine and harsh environments.

### 2. Hygrothermal-mechanical coupling and failure prediction

A major focus of my work is the coupled prediction of strength degradation and compression failure in CFRP laminates. By integrating experiments with simulation, I seek to build predictive frameworks for long-term performance assessment and life estimation.

### 3. Interface enhancement and Z-pinned composites

I am interested in through-thickness reinforcement and interface-related strengthening strategies, especially carbon Z-pins. My work explores how reinforcement parameters and service environments influence out-of-plane properties and failure mechanisms.

### 4. Multiscale finite element modeling

I use finite element methods to investigate compression failure, size effects, and damage evolution in composite structures. This includes both laminate-level simulation and structure-oriented evaluation under realistic loading conditions.

### 5. Composite manufacturing and performance optimization

I have also worked on automated fiber placement and composite structure optimization, including the effects of process parameters on porosity and mechanical behavior, as well as surrogate-model-based design optimization.

## Current Direction

At the current stage, my research is moving toward a broader integration of **environmental effects + mechanics + simulation + structure-performance design**, with the goal of supporting reliable applications of composite materials in complex engineering environments.
